title: Device Lab Evidence V1
objective: Let repository research, immutable artifact attestation, and named-device execution proceed as separate checkpoints while preserving exact evidence and support boundaries.
scope: PICO Inventory renderer receipts, Quest and Vision Pro profile research, Android artifact attestation, and independent support-claim integration
non_goals: No universal device claim, repository-stored APK trust, cross-device inference, or requirement that one contributor owns build, device, and integration

checkpoints.title: Qualify a Meta Quest Device Lab profile
checkpoints.outcome: A source-backed Quest profile and evidence boundary is reviewable without claiming a physical-device result.
non_goals: No Quest runtime result, package promotion, or PICO evidence inheritance

required_capabilities: Can compare current official Quest, OpenXR, input, privacy, and device-evidence requirements
required_tools: Web access, Git, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: A dated official-source matrix and schema-valid proposed profile

acceptance.criterion: The profile names exact supported input routes, privacy fields, evidence limits, and unresolved physical-device gates without reporting a device pass.

verification.procedure: Validate the profile and review every claim against dated official sources.
verification.expected: The profile is schema-valid and remains research-only until a physical receipt exists.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim DL-QUEST-RESEARCH on issue #29 and publish a research-only branch anchored to current official sources.

checkpoints.title: Qualify a Vision Pro Device Lab and interaction-adapter path
checkpoints.outcome: A source-backed Vision Pro spatial-input profile and adapter boundary is reviewable without claiming a physical-device result.
non_goals: No visionOS runtime result, Unity implementation, package promotion, or other-device inference

required_capabilities: Can compare current official visionOS spatial-input, accessibility, privacy, and engine-adapter guidance
required_tools: Web access, Git, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: A dated official-source matrix and schema-valid proposed profile

acceptance.criterion: The profile distinguishes gaze, pinch, hand, accessibility, privacy, engine adapter, and physical-device evidence boundaries without reporting a pass.

verification.procedure: Validate the profile and review every claim against dated official sources.
verification.expected: The profile is schema-valid and remains research-only until a physical receipt exists.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim DL-VISION-RESEARCH on issue #30 and publish a research-only branch anchored to current official sources.

checkpoints.title: Define the immutable external Android artifact contract
checkpoints.outcome: Device checkpoints can reference an externally hosted immutable Android artifact without trusting repository APK bytes or credentials.
non_goals: No artifact upload, signing authority, device execution, or automatic download of untrusted content
do_not_touch: Release signing and device support claims

required_capabilities: Can design immutable artifact identity, digest, provenance, retrieval, and trust-boundary contracts
required_tools: Git, Python 3, Android artifact format knowledge, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Positive and adversarial fixtures for immutable references, digests, identity, provenance, and untrusted retrieval

acceptance.criterion: The contract binds immutable location, SHA-256, application identity, producing commit, build tuple, provenance, retention, and trust limitations without storing APK bytes.

verification.procedure: Run valid and adversarial artifact-reference fixtures through the repository validator.
verification.expected: Valid immutable references pass and mutable, incomplete, foreign, or self-authorizing references fail closed.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Comment /claim DL-ART-01 on issue #36 and publish the contract plus adversarial fixtures on a bounded branch.

checkpoints.title: Verify Android bytes, identity, and provenance
checkpoints.outcome: A verifier reproduces artifact digest, Android application identity, Unity runtime markers, producing commit, and declared provenance before device use.
non_goals: No signing authority, malware certification, device execution, or support claim

required_capabilities: Can inspect Android packages and verify content digest, manifest identity, Unity markers, commit reachability, and provenance
required_tools: Git, Python 3, Android package inspection tools, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Positive and tampered artifact verification fixtures with independent security review

acceptance.criterion: Verification fails closed on digest, identity, runtime, commit, provenance, or immutability mismatch and records exactly what was and was not proven.

verification.procedure: Run valid and tampered Android fixtures through the verifier and full repository contract.
verification.expected: Only the exact declared artifact tuple passes and no device claim is produced.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for DL-ART-01 completion, then request Ready admission for DL-ART-02 in issue #37.

checkpoints.title: Migrate Device Lab tasks to the attested artifact route
checkpoints.outcome: Named-device tasks require an eligible attested artifact and can no longer rely on repository APK bytes or unbound build descriptions.
non_goals: No device execution, support claim, or deletion of historical receipts
do_not_touch: Repository permissions, secrets, and historical evidence

required_capabilities: Can migrate public task and receipt contracts without weakening artifact or device evidence boundaries
required_tools: Git, Python 3, GitHub Issue read access, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: End-to-end valid and missing-artifact task fixtures plus independent review

acceptance.criterion: Every named-device task binds an eligible attested artifact, and missing or stale attestation prevents Ready without erasing repo-only work.

verification.procedure: Validate migrated templates, plans, receipts, and negative missing-artifact fixtures.
verification.expected: Only tasks with eligible attested artifacts can advance to named-device Ready.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for DL-ART-02 completion, then request Ready admission for DL-ART-03 in issue #38.

checkpoints.title: Test Inventory XR UI Toolkit on PICO tracked controllers
checkpoints.outcome: A physical PICO receipt records world anchoring, readability, left-center-right controller targeting, occlusion, comfort, and semantic state for the exact UI Toolkit artifact tuple.
non_goals: No UGUI, hand, gaze, direct-poke, Quest, Vision Pro, or cross-version claim

required_capabilities: Can execute the declared world-space UI plan and record privacy-reviewed observations without broadening claims
required_tools: Eligible attested Android artifact, installation path, and receipt validator
qualification_evidence: A schema-valid physical-device receipt for the exact artifact and test-plan tuple

acceptance.criterion: The receipt records every required test-plan check, artifact digest, commit, device profile, input sources, posture, duration, failures, and non-claims.

verification.procedure: Validate the completed receipt and independently review media references and required checks.
verification.expected: The receipt supports only the exact PICO UI Toolkit tuple and records failures without promotion.

acceptance: Execute inventory-world-space-ui-v1 on a physical PICO headset with both tracked controllers for the declared minimum duration.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for DL-ART-03 and an eligible attested UI Toolkit artifact, then ask the maintainer to restore Ready on issue #27.

checkpoints.title: Test Inventory XR UGUI on PICO tracked controllers
checkpoints.outcome: A physical PICO receipt records world anchoring, readability, left-center-right controller targeting, occlusion, comfort, and semantic state for the exact UGUI artifact tuple.
non_goals: No UI Toolkit, hand, gaze, direct-poke, Quest, Vision Pro, or cross-version claim

required_capabilities: Can execute the declared world-space UI plan and record privacy-reviewed observations without broadening claims
required_tools: Eligible attested Android artifact, installation path, and receipt validator
qualification_evidence: A schema-valid physical-device receipt for the exact artifact and test-plan tuple

acceptance.criterion: The receipt records every required test-plan check, artifact digest, commit, device profile, input sources, posture, duration, failures, and non-claims.

verification.procedure: Validate the completed receipt and independently review media references and required checks.
verification.expected: The receipt supports only the exact PICO UGUI tuple and records failures without promotion.

acceptance: Execute inventory-world-space-ui-v1 on a physical PICO headset with both tracked controllers for the declared minimum duration.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for DL-ART-03 and an eligible attested UGUI artifact, then ask the maintainer to restore Ready on issue #28.

checkpoints.title: Integrate Device Lab research and named-device receipts
checkpoints.outcome: Only exact research, attestation, and device evidence that passes independent review updates profiles, support claims, and the public evidence index.
non_goals: No self-attestation, cross-device inference, or requirement that the integrator owns hardware
do_not_touch: Repository permissions, secrets, and unsupported support claims

required_capabilities: Can independently review artifact provenance, profile scope, physical-device receipts, privacy, and support-claim boundaries
required_tools: Git, Python 3, evidence retrieval, and repository validator
qualification_evidence: Independent exact-commit and exact-artifact review for every accepted claim

acceptance.criterion: Research remains research-only, every accepted device claim binds the exact profile and artifact, and unavailable hardware remains an explicit waiting boundary.

verification.procedure: Reproduce accepted artifact and receipt checks, inspect privacy-safe evidence, and run the full repository contract.
verification.expected: Only exact supported tuples update and all repository checks pass.

checkpoints.exact_next_action: Wait for all non-cancelled dependencies to complete, then request the DL-INT integration lease in issue #79.

integration.strategy: The maintainer independently reviews research, artifact attestation, privacy-safe device receipts, and exact support boundaries before integration.
